# StarRocks

[https://www.starrocks.io](https://www.starrocks.io)

![](https://img.shields.io/badge/memtables-fallback-yellow?style=flat-square) ![](https://img.shields.io/badge/inputs-StarRocks tables-blue?style=flat-square) ![](https://img.shields.io/badge/outputs-StarRocks tables | pandas | Parquet | PyArrow-orange?style=flat-square)

## Install

Install Ibis and dependencies for the StarRocks backend:

::: {.panel-tabset}

## `pip`

Install with the `starrocks` extra:

```{.bash}
pip install 'ibis-framework[starrocks]'
```

And connect:

```{.python}
import ibis

con = ibis.starrocks.connect()  # <1>
```

1. Adjust connection parameters as needed.

## `conda`

Install for StarRocks:

```{.bash}
conda install -c conda-forge ibis-starrocks
```

And connect:

```{.python}
import ibis

con = ibis.starrocks.connect()  # <1>
```

1. Adjust connection parameters as needed.

## `mamba`

Install for StarRocks:

```{.bash}
mamba install -c conda-forge ibis-starrocks
```

And connect:

```{.python}
import ibis

con = ibis.starrocks.connect()  # <1>
```

1. Adjust connection parameters as needed.

:::

## Connect

### `ibis.starrocks.connect`

```python
con = ibis.starrocks.connect(
    user="username",
    password="password",
    host="hostname",
    port=9030,
    database="database",
)
```

::: {.callout-note}
`ibis.starrocks.connect` is a thin wrapper around [`ibis.backends.starrocks.Backend.do_connect`](#ibis.backends.starrocks.Backend.do_connect).
:::

### Connection Parameters

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
from _utils import render_do_connect

render_do_connect("starrocks")
```

### `ibis.connect` URL format

In addition to `ibis.starrocks.connect`, you can also connect to StarRocks by
passing a properly-formatted StarRocks connection URL to `ibis.connect`:

```python
con = ibis.connect(f"starrocks://{user}:{password}@{host}:{port}/{database}")
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
BACKEND = "StarRocks"
```

{{< include ./_templates/api.qmd >}}
